bill.short_title: Veterans Benefits Continuity and Accountability Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2024

notes: Supplemental appropriation under Veterans Benefits Administration heading
provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for ''Compensation and Pensions'', $2,285,513,000, to remain available until expended.

notes: Supplemental appropriation under Veterans Benefits Administration heading
provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for ''Readjustment Benefits'', $596,969,000, to remain available until expended.

provisions.description: Establishes that each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
notes: Standard supplemental appropriations boilerplate ensuring funds are additive
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 101. Each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.

provisions.description: Unless otherwise provided, the additional amounts appropriated by this Act shall be available under the authorities and conditions applicable to such appropriations accounts for fiscal year 2024.
notes: Standard boilerplate applying existing FY2024 account authorities to the supplemental funds
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 102. Unless otherwise provided for by this Act, the additional amounts appropriated by this Act to appropriations accounts shall be available un

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit a report detailing corrections the Department will make to improve forecasting, data quality, and budget assumptions relating to budget submissions for Compensation and Pensions and Readjustment Benefits.
notes: Subsection (a): Budget formulation and forecasting report due within 30 days
Report to be submitted to both Appropriations and Veterans Affairs committees in both chambers
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 103. (a) Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on App

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit a report on the status of funds made available under Compensation and Pensions and Readjustment Benefits for fiscal years 2024, 2025, and 2026. Report must be updated and submitted every 90 days thereafter until September 30, 2026, including information on changes to estimates or assumptions on obligations and expenditures.
notes: Subsection (b): Recurring reporting requirement on status of funds
Proviso requires updates every 90 days through end of FY2026
Covers funds in this Act and any other Act for FY2024-2026
provisions.raw_text: (b) Not later than 60 days after the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations and th

cross_references.description: Presidential budget submission requirements; IG to recommend improvements for VA supporting information

provisions.description: Requires the Inspector General of the Department of Veterans Affairs to conduct a review of the circumstances surrounding and underlying causes of the announced VBA funding shortfall for FY2024 and the announced VHA funding shortfall for FY2025, including comparison of monthly obligations against spend plans, accuracy of projections, fund transfers and reallocations, and recommendations to improve accuracy and prevent future shortfalls. IG must submit a report within 180 days of enactment.
notes: Subsection (a): IG review of VBA FY2024 shortfall (per July 19, 2024 letter) and VHA FY2025 shortfall (per July 31, 2024 letter)
Subsection (b): Scope includes monthly obligations vs. spend plan, reasons for diversions, accuracy of projections
Subsection (c): Additional VHA-specific scope includes fund transfers, VERA process, MCAS changes
Subsection (d): Recommendations to improve accuracy of budget submissions under 31 U.S.C. 1105(a)
Subsection (e): Report due within 180 days to Appropriations and Veterans Affairs committees

flagged_issues: Supplemental bill responding to announced VA funding shortfalls in VBA (FY2024) and VHA (FY2025)
Both appropriation accounts are no-year (available until expended)
SEC. 103 and SEC. 104 impose extensive reporting and IG oversight requirements as accountability measures
